analysis_result: EED non-synonymous PASS mutation frequency in MOESM5 MAF = 1/54 = 1.9% (1 sample: Tumor_M803, Frame_Shift_Del). Note: Fig 2b oncoprint reports 13%, so the paper's EED frequency likely includes copy-number losses/additional alterations beyond the MAF-only non-synonymous mutations.
denominator_used: N = 54; the full WES cohort (MOESM4, WES=='X')
assumptions_made: Non-synonymous variant classes = Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation, Splice_Region; FILTER=='PASS' required; Tumor_Sample_Barcode constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label from MOESM4; denominator = 54 full WES cohort (not just MAF-present samples); MAF-only analysis excludes CNVs/other alterations that may be reflected in the Fig 2b oncoprint
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
  - MOESM4 (108 x 15): sample annotation; 54 have WES=='X'
  - MOESM5 (7457 x 123): WES MAF with Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, FILTER
  - MAF contains 52 unique samples (49 overlap with the 54 WES cohort; 5 WES samples have no MAF entries: M3398, 2138, 2414, 2178, 882; 3 MAF samples not in WES cohort: RT3379, 1869, M2242)

B. COHORT DEFINITION
  - WES cohort: MOESM4 where WES=='X' -> 54 samples
  - Barcodes: 'Tumor_' + WES label -> 54 barcodes
  - Denominator = 54 (full WES cohort, per Fig 2b oncoprint convention)

C. VARIABLE CODING
  - Hugo_Symbol == 'EED'
  - Variant_Classification in the 10 non-synonymous classes
  - FILTER == 'PASS' (all MAF rows in file are already PASS per FILTER column)

D. COMPUTATION
  - Restrict MAF to 54 WES cohort barcodes: 6621 rows across 49 samples
  - Apply EED + non-synonymous + PASS filters: 1 row (Tumor_M803, Frame_Shift_Del, p.R173Nfs*21)
  - Unique samples with EED mutation = 1
  - Frequency = 1/54 = 1.85%

E. RESULT
  - Overall EED mutation frequency in MOESM5 MAF = 1/54 = 1.85%
  - Paper Fig 2b reports 13% for EED. The MAF-only count (1/54) accounts for non-synonymous PASS mutations only. The paper's 13% figure likely combines mutations with copy-number loss/deletion events from the methylation-based CNV analysis, which are not captured in the MAF.

F. CAVEATS
  - MAF-only analysis; CNVs and structural variants are not in MOESM5 and are not counted
  - 5 WES samples have no MAF rows at all; denominator uses full 54
  - Fig 2b oncoprint frequency discrepancy (1.85% vs reported 13%) strongly suggests CNV/other alterations drive the paper's EED frequency
